EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER TO SHORTEN TIME TO HEAR A MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF THE HARTFORD INSURANCE COMPANY OF THE MIDWEST (FROM CONSOLIDATED CASE #CGC-12-520400)
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-10-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING, WHICH WAS UNCONTESTED, RE CROSS DEFENDANT WORLD GOLF TOUR, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. WORLD GOLF INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. WORLD GOLF INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE FIRST THREE CAUSES OF ACTION AS UNOPPOSED. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR EXPRESS INDEMNITY. WORLD GOLF INC. HAS NOT MET ITS INITIAL BURDEN ESTABLISHING AS A MATTER OF LAW IT IS NOT OBLIGATED TO INDEMNIFY BROTHERS INTERNATIONAL HOLDING CORP. PURSUANT TO THE TERMS OF THE LEASE AGREEMENT, ATTACHED AS EXHIBIT C TO THE DECLARATION OF MATTHEW S. CONANT. IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT PLAINTIFF KERRY MANSFIELD SLIPPED AND FELL IN THE STAIRWELL OF THE BUILDING. (UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACT ("UMF"), NO. 1.) IT IS FURTHER UNDISPUTED THAT MS. MANSFIELD WAS AN EMPLOYEE OF WORLD GOLF INC. AT THE TIME OF THE ACCIDENT. (UMF NO. 2.) THE INDEMNIFICATION PROVISION OF THE LEASE AGREEMENT PROVIDES IN PERTINENT PART THAT "[WORLD GOLF INC.] SHALL HOLD [BROTHERS INTERNATIONAL HOLDING CORP.] 'HARMLESS FROM AND INDEMNIFY [IT] AGAINST ANY CLAIMS, LIABILITY DAMAGES, COSTS OR EXPENSES'TO THE EXTENT ARISING FROM (A) THE ACTS OR OMISSIONS OF'[WORLD GOLF INC.'S] EMPLOYEES'.IN OR ABOUT THE REAL PROPERTY." (LEASE AGREEMENT, PAR. 14(B).) "REAL PROPERTY" IS DEFINED AS THE BUILDING AND THE LAND IT SITS ON. (LEASE AGREEMENT, PAR. 1.) HERE, THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE ESTABLISHES THAT THE INJURY RESULTED FROM AN ACT OF WORLD GOLF INC.'S EMPLOYEE WHILE IN THE BUILDING. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER AND SUBMIT IT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

REPLY IN SUPPORT OF MTN TO SEVER COMPLAINT AND X-COMPLAINT FILED BY CROSS DEFENDANT WORLD GOLF TOUR, INC.
ORDER GRANTING CROSS-DEFENDANT WORLD GOLF TOUR, INC'S MOTION TO SEVER COMPLAINT AND CROSS-COMPLAINT
MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-10-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: CROSS DEFENDANT WORLD GOLF TOUR, INC'S MOTION TO SEVER COMPLAINT AND CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE ISSUES INVOLVED IN THE UNDERLYING ACTION BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT-BROTHERS INTERNATIONAL ARE DISTINCT FROM THOSE INVOLVED IN THE ACTION BETWEEN BROTHERS INTERNATIONAL AND CROSS-DEFENDANT WORLD GOLF. MOREOVER, A FINDING THAT BROTHERS INTERNATIONAL WAS NOT NEGLIGENT IN THE UNDERLYING ACTION WOULD NEGATE THE NEED FOR ANY PARTICIPATION BY WORLD GOLF. ALTERNATIVELY, BROTHERS INTERNATIONAL WOULD NOT BE PREJUDICED BY A SUBSEQUENT TRIAL DETERMINING WORLD GOLF'S LIABILITY AS IT WOULD BE BASED ON THE WHOLLY DISTINCT ISSUE OF EXPRESS CONTRACTUAL INDEMNITY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, CROSS COMPLAINANT 300 PROSPECT PROPERTIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS MOOT IN LIGHT OF THE REQUEST FOR DISMISSAL FILED BY PLAINTIFF THE HARTFORD INSURANCE COMPANY ON NOVEMBER 24, 2014. BECAUSE NO PARTIES IN THE CONSOLIDATED MATTER ARE ASSERTING CLAIMS AGAINST CROSS-COMPLAINANT, CROSS-COMPLAINANT IS UNABLE TO ESTABLISH THAT IT IS ENTITLED TO INDEMNITY OR TO A DEFENSE. MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Jan-06-2015 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Dec-26-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS COMPLAINANT 300 PROSPECT PROPERTIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied as moot in light of the Request for Dismissal filed by Plaintiff The Hartford Insurance Company on November 24, 2014. Because no parties in the consolidated matter are asserting claims against Cross-Complainant, Cross-Complainant is unable to establish that it is entitled to indemnity or to a defense. The prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Paula J. Shi, CSR #13358 (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-09-2014 Set For Trial On Jan-26-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion Andm 0otion To Sever Complaint And X-Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-10-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel McCrary, Esq., 415.227.2323, for defendant Brothers International Holding Corp.
Kara Abelson, Esq., and Sarah Anker, Esq., 510.433.2600, for cross defendant World Golf Tour, Inc.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: CROSS DEFENDANT WORLD GOLF TOUR, INC'S Motion To Sever Complaint And Cross-Complaint is granted. The issues involved in the underlying action between Plaintiff and Defendant-Brothers International are distinct from those involved in the action between Brothers International and Cross-Defendant World Golf. Moreover, a finding that Brothers International was not negligent in the underlying action would negate the need for any participation by World Golf. Alternatively, Brothers International would not be prejudiced by a subsequent trial determining World Golf's liability as it would be based on the wholly distinct issue of express contractual indemnity. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-25-2014 Set For Case Management Conference On Jul-23-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-25-2014 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-10-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling, which was uncontested, re CROSS DEFENDANT WORLD GOLF TOUR, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. World Golf Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. World Golf Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as to the first three causes of action as unopposed. The motion is denied as to the fourth cause of action for express indemnity. World Golf Inc. has not met its initial burden establishing as a matter of law it is not obligated to indemnify Brothers International Holding Corp. pursuant to the terms of the Lease Agreement, attached as Exhibit C to the Declaration of Matthew S. Conant. It is undisputed that Plaintiff Kerry Mansfield slipped and fell in the stairwell of the building. (Undisputed Material Fact ("UMF"), no. 1.) It is further undisputed that Ms. Mansfield was an employee of World Golf Inc. at the time of the accident. (UMF no. 2.) The indemnification provision of the Lease Agreement provides in pertinent part that "[World Golf Inc.] shall hold [Brothers International Holding Corp.] 'harmless from and indemnify [it] against any claims, liability damages, costs or expenses'to the extent arising from (a) the acts or omissions of'[World Golf Inc.'s] employees'.in or about the Real Property." (Lease Agreement, par. 14(b).) "Real Property" is defined as the building and the land it sits on. (Lease Agreement, par. 1.) Here, the undisputed evidence establishes that the injury resulted from an act of World Golf Inc.'s employee while in the building. (Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
